title: Small Language Models as On-Device Retrieval Agents for Private Knowledge Work
abstract: We present a 1.3B-parameter model distilled for agentic retrieval that runs entirely on consumer laptops. By coupling a compact reranker with speculative tool-calling, the system answers multi-hop questions over local document stores at 4x lower latency than API baselines while keeping all data on-device. Human evaluation on 12 knowledge-worker tasks shows a 31% preference gain over cloud RAG for privacy-sensitive workflows.

title: Structured Extraction from Noisy Invoices with Vision-Language Models
abstract: Small-business bookkeeping still relies on brittle OCR templates. We fine-tune an open vision-language model on 40k synthetically corrupted invoices and show robust key-value extraction under skew, stamps, and handwriting. The approach reaches 96.2% field-level accuracy without per-vendor templates, suggesting template-free document automation is now feasible for long-tail SMB formats.

title: Continuous Personalization of Assistants via Lightweight Preference Adapters
abstract: Users abandon assistants that forget their preferences. We introduce per-user LoRA adapters trained online from thumbs-up/down signals, updated in under 200ms on a single GPU. Across a 6-week deployment the adapters cut repeated-correction rate by 44%, hinting that durable personal memory can ship without retraining base models.

title: Verifiable Tool Use: Certifying Agent Actions Before Execution
abstract: Autonomous agents that call external tools risk irreversible mistakes. We propose a pre-execution certificate that formally bounds the side effects of a proposed action against a user policy. On a suite of booking, payment, and email tasks the certifier blocks 98% of policy-violating actions with a 3% false-positive rate, a step toward trustworthy consumer agents.

title: Nowcasting Regional Rents from Heterogeneous Web Listings
abstract: Official rent indices lag by months. Using a de-biased ensemble over scraped listings we produce weekly rent nowcasts for 380 metros, validated against later official releases with a mean absolute error of 0.4 percentage points. The method exposes affordability shocks roughly nine weeks before published statistics.

title: Real-Time Speech Translation for Low-Resource Language Pairs on Edge Devices
abstract: We compress a cascaded speech-translation pipeline to run at conversational latency on a mobile SoC, covering 14 low-resource pairs neglected by commercial apps. A shared phoneme bottleneck lets us add a new language with two hours of audio, opening real-time translation to diaspora and travel niches ignored by incumbents.

title: Synthetic Longitudinal Health Records for Privacy-Preserving Digital Therapeutics
abstract: Startups building digital therapeutics lack longitudinal training data. We generate clinically coherent synthetic patient trajectories with a diffusion model conditioned on care guidelines, passing an expert Turing test 58% of the time. Downstream adherence-prediction models trained purely on synthetic data transfer to real cohorts within 3% AUROC.

title: Agentic Spreadsheets: Natural-Language Formulas with Provenance
abstract: Spreadsheets remain the world's most-used programming environment yet resist natural-language editing. We embed a planner that turns prose intents into audited formula chains with cell-level provenance, letting non-programmers build models they can trust. In a study of 60 analysts task completion time dropped 37% versus manual formula authoring.

title: Cheap Retrieval-Augmented Fine-Tuning for Domain-Specific Compliance Q&A
abstract: Regulated SMBs cannot afford bespoke compliance counsel. We show that retrieval-augmented fine-tuning on a 3B model over public regulatory corpora yields answers rated adequate by attorneys in 82% of audited questions, at under two cents per query, indicating a viable price point for self-serve compliance copilots.

title: Energy-Aware Scheduling for On-Prem LLM Inference in Small Data Centers
abstract: Mid-market firms increasingly self-host models but overspend on power. Our scheduler co-optimizes batching and DVFS against real-time electricity prices, cutting inference energy cost by 28% with negligible latency impact. The result lowers the total cost of owning private inference for teams unwilling to send data to cloud APIs.
